mentions.note: New CEO Steve Cahillane, architect of the value-creating Kellogg breakup, could do the same for a still-doubted Kraft Heinz.

Caller Jim asks about Kraft (merged 2011); notes Warren Buffett divesting 28% stake; Cramer says new CEO Steve Carell came from Kellogg is okay, but he and Ben Stole can't get behind it—not enough good happening in food business; Cramer is a seller, not a buyer.

Respect CEO but can't get behind it; no growth and trying to stay away from no-growth high-yield companies as yield doesn't make up for stagnation. Steve Cahillane did Kellogg breakup well so maybe, but let's give him time.
Food company considerably lower; inflation and consumer pressure on packaged foods.

Q1 upside surprise vs. low expectations; stock +2%; Cahillane (Kellogg orchestrator) investing behind 'win' brands; Power Mac, NFL partnership re-energizing; affordability focus for pressured consumer.
Faith-based play: CEO Steve Cahill (Kellogg turnaround artist) tackling legacy cost structure. 6.85% yield, high-risk; portfolio hedge if tech momentum breaks and dividend holds.
